patch.value: Profile and optimize command startup latency

Follow-up from 2026-05-10 dogfood audit: core agent-loop commands still take roughly 0.5-1.7s in an isolated temp project. Profile startup/runtime initialization, defer nonessential work for read-only commands, and add an automated dogfood benchmark with thresholds.

A benchmark covers init/create/list/context/search/calendar/health; startup hot paths are profiled; read-only commands avoid nonessential extension/telemetry/vector work; representative local commands feel sub-second where feasible.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: ran pm search startup performance instant command benchmark lazy initialization --limit 10. Only closed historical performance/search items matched; no open item covers current command startup latency benchmark work.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Dogfood final timings on 2026-05-10 still showed init 1387ms, create about 1.1s, list-open 1209ms, context 1051ms, keyword search 1015ms, calendar 1411ms, health 1754ms.

value.note: detached telemetry flush keeps CLI responsive

patch.value: detached telemetry flush keeps CLI responsive

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: CI follow-up: GitHub run 25641271233 failed only on macOS Node 20 in tests/unit/comments-command.spec.ts with ENOTEMPTY while removing the temporary .agents/pm tree. Added a bounded ENOTEMPTY/EBUSY/EPERM retry to tests/helpers/withTempPmPath.ts and kept the in-progress incremental semantic reindex + lock-directory guard changes. Local focused verification passed: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/comments-command.spec.ts tests/unit/reindex-command.spec.ts tests/unit/telemetry-runtime.spec.ts tests/integration/release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts (72 tests).

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Evidence: latest audit/performance pass completed on 2026-05-10. Changes shipped in 24731a2 and 66e0647 keep production telemetry responsive via detached flush worker, add reindex locking, create missing lock dirs defensively, and skip unchanged semantic embeddings. Temp-project dogfood passed init/create/claim/update/comment/files/docs/test/list/context/search/calendar/aggregate/dedupe/contracts/extension/health/validate/close/release with sandboxed PM_PATH and PM_GLOBAL_PATH. Startup benchmark after the telemetry change: pm --version 608ms, init 597ms, list-open 655ms, context 730ms, calendar 635ms, search 835ms, health 665ms with queue drained. Hybrid reindex on current tracker: 686 loaded, 686 unchanged embeddings skipped, completed in 869ms. Verification passed: focused reindex/telemetry/release-readiness tests, lock coverage tests, search-cache focused test, serial full coverage 90 files/1276 tests at 100%, pnpm quality:static, pnpm quality:docs-skills, node scripts/check-secrets.mjs, git diff --check, and main CI run 25641740951 green across macOS/Windows/Ubuntu matrix. Health/validate remaining findings are pre-existing tracker hygiene warnings: history drift on five legacy items and one resolution metadata warning. Remote telemetry stack was live with queue depth 0, active consumer, and recent events; Sentry still has unresolved historical groups including a scrubbed latest high-priority import error.

author: codex-agent

patch.value: Optimized startup telemetry flushing and semantic reindex hot path; added lock-dir resilience and CI cleanup stability.
Common pm commands stay responsive, unchanged semantic reindex avoids redundant embedding work, and regression coverage plus CI stay green.
Default telemetry benchmark commands complete in roughly 0.6-0.8s, unchanged hybrid reindex completes in 869ms for 686 items, coverage/static/docs/secrets/CI passed.

message: Record closure metadata for startup and reindex performance audit

patch.value: Startup telemetry and semantic reindex performance optimized; dogfood, coverage, static quality, secret scan, telemetry/Sentry review, and main CI passed.
